text:
Once upon a time in a small Italian village, there lived an old tailor named Aldo. Aldo was loved by everyone in town for his masterful dresses from feathers and wool. One sunny afternoon, while stitching an especially intricate cape for Dulcie the deer, he heard a wee little voice.

"There oughta be laws," the voice said.

Aldo looked down. It was a tiny girl with long braided hair. Her name was Celesta.

“You love knitting!” Celesta said.

“Even cats?” Aldo smiled.

“Or flying squirrels.”

He laughed. “Well, I’ve got just the thing!”

That night, he made her the fanciestowl ever embroidered cloak. They sat beside the fire. Celesta giggled at the feathers.

“Why do you fly?”

“To hear music… I think.”

“Oh.”

* * *

A few moons later. Celesta flew off alone. Aldo searched owlhoots. They found feathers.
